zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Bernake於1356年出生在英國林可爾史爾塔特沙拉爾時，她的父親William Bernanke26歲，母親Mary Engaine21歲。她於1370年與Sir James De Bellers在英國林可爾史爾塔特沙拉爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1383年在英國萊希斯特斯爾梅爾敦 莫布雷享年27歲。

nameMeanings.name: Bernake
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Jaime, Pedro, Salvador, Alberto, Jose Luis, Juan, Luis, Roberto, Carlos, Damaso, Emilio.  Italian Antonio, Aldo, Angelo, Cecilio, Filiberto, Lucio, Marco.
Spanish and French (Bernabé); Italian (Bernabè): from local forms of the Biblical personal name Barnabas (see Barnaby ).

variants.name: Barnaby
meanings: from the Middle English vernacular form of the Greek and Latin personal name Barnabas, which was borne by the companion of St. Paul (Acts 4:36). This is of Aramaic origin, from Aramaic bar naḅyā ‘son of the prophet’, although the Greek text of Acts 4:36 explains the name as hyios paraklēseōs ‘son of consolation’ or ‘encouragement’.

habitational name from any of the four places: Barnaby in North Yorkshire, Barnetby le Wold or Barnoldby le Beck (both in Lincolnshire), or Barnby in Suffolk. Barnaby in North Yorkshire is named with the Old English personal name Beornwald (composed of the elements beorn ‘young warrior’ + wald ‘rule’) + Old Norse bȳ ‘settlement, farmstead’. Barnby in Suffolk is named with Old Norse barn ‘child, offspring’ + bȳ meaning ‘children's farm’, perhaps for an estate divided by heirs.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Bernake was born in 1356, in Tattershall, Lincolnshire, England, her father, William Bernanke, was 26 and her mother, Mary Engaine, was 21. She married Sir James De Bellers in 1370, in Tattershall, Lincolnshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1383, in Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, England, at the age of 27.

Elle a épousé Sir James De Bellers en 1370, à Tattershall, Lincolnshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1383, à Melton Mowbray, Leicestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 27 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Bernake(이)가 1356년 영국 링코실르 타터샤에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Bernanke(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary Engaine(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1370년 영국 링코실르 타터샤에서 Sir James De Bellers(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1383년 영국 레이스스터실르 멜턴 모우브라이에서 27세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Bernake родилась в 1356 г. в местности таттиршолл, линколншир, Англия, её отцу по имени William Bernanke было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Engaine, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir James De Bellers в 1370 г. в местности таттиршолл, линколншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1383 г. в местности мелтон мавбрэи, лисестиршир, Англия в возрасте 27 лет.
